zh.lifeSummary: Sir Mauger St Aubyn III是Stephen de Sancto Albino的兒子，於大約1254年出生在英國戴文。他和Isabella Pidekeswell至少有4個兒子和2個女兒。他去世於大約1294年在英國戴文格奧智哈姆享年42歲，埋葬在英國戴文格奧智哈姆。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Sir Mauger St Aubyn III是Stephen de Sancto Albino的儿子，于大约1254年出生在英国戴文。他和Isabella Pidekeswell至少有4个儿子和2个女儿。他去世于大约1294年在英国戴文格奧智哈姆享年42岁，埋葬在英国戴文格奧智哈姆。

nameMeanings.name: St
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: 1  English (of Norman origin): habitational name from any of the numerous places in France so called from the dedication of their churches to St. Jean (  see  John  ).
2  Americanized form of French   St  .   Jean  .

variants.name: John
meanings: English and Welsh: ultimately from the Hebrew personal name Yoḥanan ‘Jehovah has favored (me with a son)’ or ‘may Jehovah favor (this child)’. This personal name was adopted into Latin (via Greek) as Johannes, and has enjoyed enormous popularity in Europe throughout the Christian era, being given in honor of Saint John the Baptist, precursor of Christ, and of Saint John the Evangelist, author of the fourth gospel, as well as the nearly one thousand other Christian saints who bore the name. Some of the principal forms of the personal name in other languages are: Welsh Ieuan, Evan, Siôn, and Ioan; Scottish Ia(i)n; Irish Séan; German Johann, Johannes; Dutch and Slavic Jan; French Jean; Italian Giovanni; Spanish Juan; Portuguese João; Greek Iōannēs (vernacular Giannis, Yannis); Russian Ivan. There were a number of different forms of the name in Middle English, including Jan(e), a male name (see Jayne ); Jen (see Jenkin ); Jon(e) (see Jones ); and Han(n) (see Hann ). By the beginning of the 14th century John rivalled William in popularity and has always been a favorite name. Johan became Jo(h)n, and another Old French form Jehan was shortened to Jan and Jen, giving rise to Old French and Middle English diminutives such as Jonin, Janin, and Jenin. More common in Middle English were Jankin, Jonkin, and Jenkin, which were Middle Dutch pet forms introduced after the Conquest by Flemish and Picard settlers. The most common pet form of John was Jack, another borrowing from Flemish and Picard usage. Han may sometimes have been a short form of Johan but was more usually a pet form of Henry. There were also various Middle English feminine versions of this name (e.g. Joan, Jehan), some of which were indistinguishable from masculine forms. The distinction on grounds of gender between John and Joan was not firmly established in English until the 17th century. It was even later that Jean and Jane were specialized as specifically female names in English; bearers of these surnames and their derivatives are more likely to derive them from a male ancestor than a female. As a surname in the British Isles, John is particularly frequent in Wales, where it is a late formation representing Welsh Siôn rather than the older form Ieuan (which gave rise to the surname Evan ). In North America, the English form of the surname has absorbed many cognates from other languages, e.g. Assyrian/Chaldean Youkhana , French Jean , Hungarian János (see Janos ), Slovenian Janež and Janeš (see Janes ), Czech Jan , Albanian Gjoni , and their derivatives (see examples at Johnson ). The name John is also found among Christians in southern India (compare Ninan and Yohannan ), but since South Indians traditionally do not have hereditary surnames, the southern Indian name was in most cases registered as such only after immigration of its bearers to the US.
German: from a North German and Silesian variant of the personal name   Johannes  . This surname is also found in France (Alsace and Lorraine). Compare   Yohn  .

variants.name: Jean
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Pierre, Francois, Marcel, Yves, Andre, Jacques, Georges, Michel, Philippe, Serge, Yvon, Cecile.

French, Walloon, West Indian (mainly Haiti), Mauritian, and English (of French Huguenot origin): from the male personal name Jean, French form of John . This is one of the most frequent surnames in Haiti (along with Joseph and Pierre ). It is also established elsewhere in the West Indies (e.g. in the Dominican Republic, Saint Lucia, Saint Martin, and Bahamas).
English: habitational name for someone from Genoa in Italy; compare   Janeway  .

nameMeanings.name: Aubyn
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Lucien, Andre, Normand, Armand, Cecile, Emile, Henri, Monique, Adelard, Aime, Alcid, Aldege.

French: from the personal name Aubin (from Latin Albinus, a derivative of albus ‘white’). This was the name of several minor early Christian saints, including a famous bishop of Angers (died c. 554). At an early date, this name became confused with the ancient Germanic personal name Albuin (see Albin ). Compare Obey and Obin .
English: variant of   Albin  .

variants.name: Albin
meanings: English, French, and German: from the personal name Albin (from Latin Albinus, a derivative of albus ‘white’). In England this is generally a variant of Alban . The usual spelling of the French name is Aubin . The personal name was especially popular in Austria, Lombardy, and Savoy, where it absorbed the ancient Germanic name Albuin (which is composed of the elements alb ‘elf’ + win ‘friend’; compare Alvin ). This was the name of the Lombard leader (died 572) who made himself king of northern Italy, and also of various Christian saints, including a bishop of Brixen (Bressanone) in South Tyrol.
Galician (Albín): habitational name from any of the four places called Albín in Galicia (Spain), from Latin (villa) Albini.

nameMeanings.name: Mauger
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: French and Channel Islands (Guernsey and Jersey): from the Old French (Norman) personal name Maug(i)er, Malg(i)er, from ancient Germanic Madalgar(i), Malger, composed of the elements madal ‘council, meeting’ and gar(i) ‘spear’, or from Amalgar(i), with the first element amal ‘capable, brave, eager’. Compare Major , Monger , and Munger .
Probably also an altered form of German   Mager  .

variants.name: Maugeri
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Carmine, Ignazio, Salvatore, Angelo, Camillo, Giuseppe, Orazio, Pietro.
Italian (southern):
variant of Malgeri, from the ancient Germanic personal name Madalgari or Amalgari (see Mauger ).

variants.name: Major
meanings: English (of Norman origin): from the personal name Malg(i)er, Maug(i)er, an Old French form of ancient Germanic Madalgar(i) (see Mauger ).
Hungarian: from a shortened form of majorosgazda (see Majoros ) or a derivative of German Meyer .
Polish, Czech, and Slovak: from the military rank major (derived from Latin maior ‘greater’), a word related to English mayor and the German surname Meyer .

variants.name: Mangieri
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Gaspar, Mario, Angelo, Arsenio, Mafalda, Orazio, Rocco, Silvio.
Italian: variant of Malgeri, from the ancient Germanic personal name Madalgari or Amalgari (see Mauger ).

historicEvents.title: 100 Years of Conflict Between England and France Ends
historicEvents.description: Treaty of Paris was signed on December 4, 1259, which ended 100 years of conflict between England and France.

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

en.lifeSummary: Sir Mauger St Aubyn III was born about 1254, in Devon, England as the son of Stephen de Sancto Albino. He had at least 4 sons and 2 daughters with Isabella Pidekeswell. He died about 1294, in Georgeham, Devon, England, at the age of 42, and was buried in Georgeham, Devon, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Sir Mauger St Aubyn III est né environ 1254, à Devon, Angleterre, fils de Stephen de Sancto Albino. Il a eu au moins 4 fils et 2 filles avec Isabella Pidekeswell. Il est décédé environ 1294, à Georgeham, Devon, Angleterre, à l’âge de 42 ans, et a été enterré à Georgeham, Devon, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Sir Mauger St Aubyn III wurde um 1254 in Devon, England, als Sohn von Stephen de Sancto Albino geboren. Er hatte mit Isabella Pidekeswell mindestens 4 Söhne und 2 Töchter. Er starb um 1294 im Alter von 42 Jahren in Georgeham, Devon, England und wurde in Georgeham, Devon, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Sir Mauger St Aubyn III è nato il circa 1254 nel Devon, England come figlio di Stephen de Sancto Albino. Lui avuto almeno 4 figli e 2 figlie con Isabella Pidekeswell. Morì circa 1294, nel Georgeham, Devon, England, all'età di 42 anni, e fu sepolto a Georgeham, Devon, England.

ja.Georgeham, Devon, England: イギリスデボンジョージェハム
ja.lifeSummary: Sir Mauger St Aubyn IIIは約1254年にイギリスデボンでStephen de Sancto Albinoの息子として生まれた。彼はIsabella Pidekeswellとの間に少なくとも4人の息子と2人の娘をもうけた。彼は約1294年にイギリスデボンジョージェハムで42歳で死去し，イギリスデボンジョージェハムにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Sir Mauger St Aubyn III(은)는 대략 1254년 영국 데폰에서 Stephen de Sancto Albino의 아들로 태어났다. 그는 Isabella Pidekeswell(와)과 적어도 네 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 대략 1294년 영국 데폰 조그함에서 42세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 데폰 조그함에 묻혔다.

mg.about 1254–about 1294: tany ho any amin'ny 1254–tany ho any amin'ny 1294

pt.lifeSummary: Sir Mauger St Aubyn III nasceu aproximadamente 1254, em Devon, Inglaterra filho de Stephen de Sancto Albino. Ele teve pelo menos 4 filhos e 2 filhas com Isabella Pidekeswell. Ele faleceu aproximadamente 1294, em Georgeham, Devon, Inglaterra, com 42 anos, e foi sepultado em Georgeham, Devon, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Sir Mauger St Aubyn III родился около 1254 г. в местности девон, Англия. Его отца звали Stephen de Sancto Albino. У него было по крайней мере 4 сына и 2 дочери от Isabella Pidekeswell. Он умер около 1294 г. в местности джеоргэам, девон, Англия в возрасте 42 лет и был похоронен в местности джеоргэам, девон, Англия.

sm.about 1254–about 1294: e tusa o le 1254–e tusa o le 1294

es.lifeSummary: Sir Mauger St Aubyn III nació aproximadamente 1254, en Devon, Inglaterra como hijo de Stephen de Sancto Albino. Tuvo por lo menos 4 hijos y 2 hijas con Isabella Pidekeswell. Murió aproximadamente 1294, en Georgeham, Devon, Inglaterra, a la edad de 42 años, y fue sepultado en Georgeham, Devon, Inglaterra.
